channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Nintendo Suing United States Government Over Tariff
item.content:encoded:
As first reported by Nicole Carpenter of Aftermath, Nintendo of America is suing the United States government over the broad tariff policy enacted by President Donald Trump in 2025. The complaint, filed in the U.S. Court of International Trade, claims that since February 2025, the Trump Administration has imposed unlawful tariffs, leading to the collection of over $200 billion in tariffs on imports from nearly all countries.

In the complaint, Nintendo‘s legal counsel says the gaming company has “standing to sue” because it imports Goods that were subject to IEEPA Duties. Therefore, they’re seeking a refund with interest on the tariffs they paid. However, a specific amount was not stated. Alternatively, they would want to enter a money judgment for the amount. They’re also asking for reasonable attorney fees.

More on the Tariffs and Nintendo

On February 20, the US Supreme Court found the tariffs to be unconstitutional. Subsequently, New York Judge Richard Eaton of the U.S. Court of International Trade stated that all importers of record were entitled to benefit following the ruling. Eaton said he will be the one hearing cases involving refunds.

President Trump later said that he would impose new 15% tariffs, an increase from the previous tariffs.

Nintendo launched its Switch 2 console shortly after the tariffs were announced, and the tariffs actually delayed United States pre-orders for the hardware.

Those interested can read Nintendo’s full complaint at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to bring you the latest on this story as more develops.

What do you think about this move that sees Nintendo of America sue the United States government? Do you think the price of the Nintendo Switch 2 will rise in the near future due to tariff concerns and /or RAM issues? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: South of Midnight Releasing on PS5 This Month
item.content:encoded:
South of Midnight has a PlayStation 5 release date.

Sony released a new trailer confirming that South of Midnight for the PlayStation 5 will be available on March 31. The game was originally an Xbox and PC exclusive in 2025. It’s still available on Game Pass for those subscribers.

In the game, players take control of Hazel. She’s a Weaver who repairs broken bonds and spirits. The game explores Southern Folklore mythos and creatures in a dark, fantastical world. Hazel fights dangerous beings while uncovering family secrets. She must rescue her mother, save her hometown, and use her powers to heal creatures and reveal their traumas. The game features diverse Southern regions, such as the decayed Prospero County.

Xbox Has Found Great Success as a Third-Party Publisher

Xbox’s many game studios have begun releasing titles on other platforms. Indiana Jones and the Great Circle has found great success on the PlayStation 5. Halo is also coming to PlayStation for the first time with a remake of the original Halo campaign. The next title in the Fable franchise is also heading to PlayStation.

Xbox Game Studios recently spoke about the strategy behind releasing Xbox games on PlayStation, noting the inconsistency in which releases land on other platforms. Read the full comments here.

Asha Sharma, the new Xbox CEO, continues to express excitement about the “return of Xbox.” Fans can read what she means by that sentiment here.

South of Midnight is also heading to the Nintendo Switch 2. Insider Gaming will continue to provide updates on the future of Xbox.

Are you excited to play South of Midnight on PlayStation 5? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
